tldr: Move from New Relic's hosted APM/observability suite to Checkmk, an infrastructure and application monitoring platform with an open-source raw edition. Plan to rebuild dashboards and alerts since the data models and query approaches are different.
why_switch: Avoid New Relic's commit-tier minimums by self-hosting
Checkmk has a free OSS edition for cost control
Lower lock-in via a more portable monitoring model
Strong fit if infrastructure monitoring is the core need
On-prem or self-hosted deployment options available
what_to_export: APM service inventory and language agents in use
Alert policies, conditions, and notification channels
Log pipelines and parsing rules (if used)
export_method: Use the New Relic NerdGraph API to list and export dashboards, alert policies, conditions, synthetics, and entity metadata as JSON. Store the exports in a Git repo so you have an auditable record before any change in production. For metric and event history, run NRQL queries over the windows you must retain and persist the results to object storage; full historical backfill is usually impractical due to retention and rate limits, so most teams keep New Relic read-only for a transition window. Export users, teams, and roles via the admin API. Document NRQL queries that drive critical alerts and dashboards because they will need to be reformulated against the new tool. Capture the list of active language agents and infra integrations deployed across hosts and Kubernetes clusters so you can plan replacements. If you depend on logs, export pipeline rules and sampling configuration. Keep New Relic agents running during the parallel period to avoid blind spots.
import_method: Install a Checkmk site (Raw or Enterprise) and roll out the Checkmk agent to your hosts and containers. Configure host groups, services, and check plugins to cover the inventory you exported from New Relic. Recreate alert rules using Checkmk's notification rules and wire them to the same email, Slack, or PagerDuty channels you used previously. Rebuild key dashboards in Checkmk's views and graphs; treat the New Relic export as a reference only since query languages differ. For application-level signals that Checkmk does not natively replace, plan a complementary tool (for example, an OpenTelemetry-based tracer) rather than expecting full APM parity. Validate by running both systems in parallel and comparing alert and uptime behaviour for at least one business cycle. Recreate users, contact groups, and roles in Checkmk and connect SSO to your IdP. Once parity is confirmed, remove New Relic agents and end the contract at the next renewal point.
gotchas: Checkmk's primary strength is infrastructure monitoring; full APM parity may need an additional tool
Self-hosting Checkmk adds operational responsibility (backups, upgrades, sizing)
NRQL does not translate directly; plan a dashboard rebuild
New Relic commit tiers may keep billing in force until the contract end date
Notification payload formats may need template updates downstream
Run both systems in parallel until alert parity is confirmed

email.subject: Notice: ending our New Relic subscription within 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our monitoring to a self-hosted setup and will not be renewing New Relic. Please treat this email as our 14-day notice to end the subscription at the next available term and to disable auto-renewal.

Kindly confirm the effective end date, the final invoice, and any data export options that remain available to us. We will keep agents running until cutover completes.

Thanks for the support over the contract period.

Best regards,
Your customer
